title: Probabilistically nilpotent Hopf algebras
journal: Transactions of the American Mathematical Society
abstract:
In this paper we investigate nilpotenct and probabilistically nilpotent Hopf algebras. We define nilpotency via a descending chain of commutators and give a criterion for nilpotency via a family of central invertible elements. These elements can be obtained from a commutator matrix 

  
    A
    A
  

 which depends only on the Grothendieck ring of 

  
    
      H
      .
    
    H.
  

 When 

  
    H
    H
  

 is almost cocommutative we introduce a probabilistic method. We prove that every semisimple quasitriangular Hopf algebra is probabilistically nilpotent. In a sense we thereby answer the title of our paper Are we counting or measuring anything? by Yes, we are.
